figures.caption: Table 1: Results on the CALVIN [34] and LIBERO [29] benchmarks. Best results are highlighted in bold. Baselines marked with ∗ denote reproduced or re-trained implementations evaluated under the same training and evaluation pipeline as AnchorRefine.
Figure 5: Overview of the evaluation benchmarks, including CALVIN, LIBERO, and the Real-world manipulation tasks.
Figure 8: Representative success cases from the Real-world experiments.
Figure 7: Qualitative comparison on a contact-sensitive task from LIBERO-Long.

Figure 4: Real-world evaluation on the LeRobot SO101 platform across four representative manipulation tasks. For each task, we report the task success rate, while the rightmost panel summarizes the average success rate across all tasks.

Figure 2: Overview of AnchorRefine. AnchorRefine decomposes action generation into two components: a Trajectory Anchor Planner predicting a coarse anchor trajectory in the original action space, and a Residual Refine Module learning fine-grained corrections in a residual space relative to the anchor. The final action combines anchor and residual predictions. Training proceeds sequentially: first learning the anchor planner, then freezing it to train the refinement module.
